Conditions A to Z
Poisoning 
Poisoning can happen if you swallow, touch, or breathe in substances that can be harmful, such as chemicals or medicines. Get medical advice immediately if you think you or someone else has been poisoned.
Immediate action required: Call 999 or go to A&E if:
you think you, a child, or someone else may have swallowed, touched or breathed in something harmful someone has lost consciousness someone has stopped breathing you or someone else has severe difficulty breathing - you're unable to get words out, or you're choking or gasping you or someone else has a seizure (fit) 
Find your nearest A&E 
Information: 
Do not drive to A&E. Ask someone to drive you or call 999 and ask for an ambulance.
Bring any medicines you take with you.
Urgent advice: Call NHS 111 if:
you're not sure if something you or someone else have swallowed, touched or breathed in is harmful 
While you're waiting for medical help 
If you think you or someone else may have been poisoned, there are some things you can do while you're waiting for medical help.
Do
start CPR if someone is unresponsive and not breathing
put the person in the recovery position if they'
